modules.name: tangled_agent.main
documentation: Main entry point for the Tangled agent game playing tools.

functions.name: tangled_agent.main.import_agent
documentation:
Dynamically import a class from a string specification in the format 'module_name.ClassName'.

Args:
    agent_name (str): The module and class name as a single string.

Returns:
    type: The class type that was dynamically imported.

functions.name: tangled_agent.main.generate_sample_agent
documentation: Generate a sample agent class with the given name.

functions.name: tangled_agent.main.main
documentation:
Main entry point for the script.
Get the command-line arguments and start the game.

classes.name: tangled_agent.random_agent.RandomRandyAgent

methods.name: tangled_agent.random_agent.RandomRandyAgent.make_move
documentation:
Make a move in the game.
game: Game: The game instance

Returns a tuple of the move type, move index, and move state.

This is an example of a simple RandomRandyAgent that makes random moves.

Use this template to build your own agents. Here are the basic steps:

1. Import the GameAgentBase class from tangled_game_engine.tangled_game_agent.base_agent.
    This will be the base class for your agent.
2. Import the Game class from tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.
    This will be the state of the game that your agent will interact with. See the class for more details,
    but generally you'll have access to the full state of the game (the vertices and edges and their states)
    and some helpful methods for interacting with the game (get_legal_moves, etc.)
3. Create a new class that inherits from GameAgentBase and implement the make_move method.
    The make_move method should take a Game object as an argument and return a tuple of the move type,
    move index, and move state.
    move_type is Game.MoveType IntEnum, and has values of VERTEX, EDGE, or QUIT.
    move_index is the index of the vertex or edge to change the state of.
    move_state is the state to change the vertex or edge.
        for VERTEX: 
            Vertex.State.P1
            Vertex.State.P2
            (Vertex.State.NONE is the initial state)
        for EDGE: 
            Edge.State.NEITHER
            Edge.State.FM
            Edge.State.AFM
            (Edge.State.NONE is the initial state)

    The move should be returned as a tuple of these three values as integers.
    e.g. (Game.MoveType.VERTEX.value, 3, Vertex.State.P1.value)

classes.name: tangled_agent.sample_agent.SampleAgent

methods.name: tangled_agent.sample_agent.SampleAgent.__init__
documentation: Do custom initialization of your game agent. Do not use command line arguments here.

methods.name: tangled_agent.sample_agent.SampleAgent.add_cli_options
documentation:
Hook for agents to add their CLI options. Override for subclasses that need custom cli options.

Example:        
    parser.add_argument('--my_custom_option', type=int, default=0, help='My custom option')
    
Args:
    parser (configargparse.ArgParser): The argument parser instance to add options to.

methods.name: tangled_agent.sample_agent.SampleAgent.on_game_start
documentation:
Called when a game starts. This is a good place to do any setup that is needed for the agent.
This method can be overridden by a subclass if needed. Use any custom options set in add_cli_options here.

Example:
    self.my_custom_option = args.my_custom_option
    
Args:
    game (Game): The game object that has started.
    **kwargs: Any additional arguments that are passed to the agent.

methods.name: tangled_agent.sample_agent.SampleAgent.make_move
documentation:
Make a move in the game.
game: Game: The game instance

Returns a tuple of the move type, move index, and move state.

This is an example of a simple SampleAgent that makes random moves.

Use this template to build your own agents. Here are the basic steps:

1. Import the GameAgentBase class from tangled_game_engine.tangled_game_agent.base_agent.
    This will be the base class for your agent.
2. Import the Game class from tangled_game_engine.tangled_game.
    This will be the state of the game that your agent will interact with. See the class for more details,
    but generally you'll have access to the full state of the game (the vertices and edges and their states)
    and some helpful methods for interacting with the game (get_legal_moves, etc.)
3. Create a new class that inherits from GameAgentBase and implement the make_move method.
    The make_move method should take a Game object as an argument and return a tuple of the move type,
    move index, and move state.
    move_type is Game.MoveType IntEnum, and has values of VERTEX, EDGE, or QUIT.
    move_index is the index of the vertex or edge to change the state of.
    move_state is the state to change the vertex or edge.
        for VERTEX: 
            Vertex.State.P1
            Vertex.State.P2
            (Vertex.State.NONE is the initial state)
        for EDGE: 
            Edge.State.NEITHER
            Edge.State.FM
            Edge.State.AFM
            (Edge.State.NONE is the initial state)

    The move should be returned as a tuple of these three values as integers.
    e.g. (Game.MoveType.VERTEX.value, 3, Vertex.State.P1.value)

modules.name: tangled_agent.base_agent
documentation:
Base class for game agents.
Create a subclass of this class to implement a game agent.

classes.name: tangled_agent.base_agent.GameAgentBase

methods.name: tangled_agent.base_agent.GameAgentBase.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the game agent with the player id.
This id must either match an id of the player in a game, or the game must allow all players.

Args:
    player_id (str): The player id for this agent.

methods.name: tangled_agent.base_agent.GameAgentBase.add_cli_options
documentation:
Hook for agents to add their CLI options. Override for subclasses that need custom cli options.
Args:
    parser (ArgumentParser): The argument parser instance to add options to.

methods.name: tangled_agent.base_agent.GameAgentBase.on_game_start
documentation:
Called when a game starts. This is a good place to do any setup that is needed for the agent.
This method can be overridden by a subclass if needed.

Args:
    game (Game): The game object that has started.
    **kwargs: Any additional arguments that are passed to the agent.

methods.name: tangled_agent.base_agent.GameAgentBase.make_move
documentation:
Have the agent make a move in the game. This method should be implemented by a subclass.
This will be called each time it is the agent's turn to make a move.
The move must be a valid move for the game and the agent.

Args:
    game (Game): The game object to make a move in. Use this to get valid moves and the current state of the game.

Returns:
    Tuple[int, int, int]: A tuple of the move type (see Game.MoveType), move index (vertex or edge), and move state (see Vertex.State and Edge.State).

Base class for game agents.
Create a subclass of this class to implement a game agent.

functions.name: tangled_agent.basic_game.play_local_game
documentation:
Sets up and starts a local game with two random agents.

Args:
    player1 (GameAgentBase): The first player agent.
    player2 (GameAgentBase): The second player agent.
    game (Game): The game instance to play.

functions.name: tangled_agent.basic_game.play_remote_game
documentation:
Sets up and starts a remote game with a random agent.

Args:
    game_id (str): The ID of the game to connect to (provided by create_game on the website).
    host (str): The host URL for the remote game server (provided by create_game on the website).
    player (GameAgentBase): The player agent to use.

methods.name: tangled_agent.graph_display.GraphGameGUI.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the GUI window with the given resolution.

Args:
    resolution (Tuple[int, int]): Width and height of the window (default: 800x600)

methods.name: tangled_agent.graph_display.GraphGameGUI.__del__
documentation: Destructor to ensure the window is closed when the instance is destroyed.

methods.name: tangled_agent.graph_display.GraphGameGUI.on_resize
documentation: Handle window resize by redrawing the graph.

methods.name: tangled_agent.graph_display.GraphGameGUI.calculate_node_positions
documentation: Calculate positions for nodes in a circular layout.

methods.name: tangled_agent.graph_display.GraphGameGUI.draw_node
documentation: Draw a node with appropriate color based on player ownership.

methods.name: tangled_agent.graph_display.GraphGameGUI.draw_edge
documentation: Draw an edge with appropriate color based on its state.

methods.name: tangled_agent.graph_display.GraphGameGUI.draw_legend
documentation: Draw a legend showing edge state colors.

methods.name: tangled_agent.graph_display.GraphGameGUI.update
documentation:
Update the display with new game state.
This method should be called whenever the game state changes.

Args:
    game_state (Dict[str, Any]): Current game state

methods.name: tangled_agent.graph_display.GraphGameGUI.display_status
documentation:
Update the display with new game state and enforce a minimum delay between updates.

Args:
    game_state (Dict[str, Any]): Current game state
    delay (float): Minimum time in seconds that should elapse between display updates

documentation: Find all implementations of the GameAgentBase class in the tangled_agent module and return their names.

documentation: Create an agent by name.

documentation: Update the terminal dimensions safely across platforms.

methods.name: tangled_agent.pretty_game.GameDisplay.clear_screen
documentation: Clear the screen in a cross-platform way.

methods.name: tangled_agent.pretty_game.GameDisplay.count_edge_states

modules.name: tangled_agent.base_game_player
documentation:
The base class for a game player. This is used in this package to 
make a local game player that plays a game locally between two agents.

classes.name: tangled_agent.base_game_player.GamePlayerBase

methods.name: tangled_agent.base_game_player.GamePlayerBase.play_game
documentation:
Implement this function to play the game and return the final state.

Returns:
    dict: The final state of the game.
        (see Game.get_game_state() for the format)

methods.name: tangled_agent.base_game_player.GamePlayerBase.start_game
documentation:
Starts the game client with the specified subclass.

Args:
    client_class (Type[GamePlayerBase]): The subclass of GamePlayerBase to start.
    *args, **kwargs: Additional arguments for the subclass constructor.

The base class for a game player. This is used in this package to
make a local game player that plays a game locally between two agents.

It can be extended to create a game player for remote game services
or other situations.

modules.name: tangled_agent.remote_game_player
documentation: This module provides the RemoteGamePlayer class, which manages a player's interaction with a remote game server.

classes.name: tangled_agent.remote_game_player.RemoteGamePlayer

methods.name: tangled_agent.remote_game_player.RemoteGamePlayer.authenticate
documentation:
Authenticate the user with Auth0
Save this authentication so we don't have to regenerate it every time

methods.name: tangled_agent.remote_game_player.RemoteGamePlayer.play_game
documentation: Plays the game until it is over.

methods.name: tangled_agent.remote_game_player.RemoteGamePlayer.__create_a_test_game
documentation:
Create a test game for the player.
This shouldn't be part of the final package, but is useful for testing.

methods.name: tangled_agent.remote_game_player.RemoteGamePlayer.join_game
documentation:
Set up connection to the server to join the remote game.
Saves the player connection token and player index.

methods.name: tangled_agent.remote_game_player.RemoteGamePlayer.get_game_state
documentation: Set up connection to the server to get the game state

methods.name: tangled_agent.remote_game_player.RemoteGamePlayer.make_move
documentation: Set up connection to the server to make a move

methods.name: tangled_agent.remote_game_player.RemoteGamePlayer.__test_token_and_login
documentation: Test the login process.

methods.name: tangled_agent.remote_game_player.RemoteGamePlayer.decode_token
documentation:
Decode a JWT token to extract claims.

Args:
    token (str): The JWT token to decode.

Returns:
    dict: The decoded claims.

methods.name: tangled_agent.remote_game_player.RemoteGamePlayer.validate_token
documentation:
Decode a JWT token to extract claims.

Args:
    token (str): The JWT token to decode.

Returns:
    dict: The decoded claims.

methods.name: tangled_agent.remote_game_player.RemoteGamePlayer.get_user_id
documentation:
Get the user ID from the token.

Args:
    token (str): The JWT token to decode.

Returns:
    str: The user ID.

Represents a player in a remote game, providing methods to authenticate, join a game,
make moves, and synchronize the game state with a remote server.

Attributes:
    player (GameAgentBase): The agent representing the player.
    agent_name (str): The name of the player's agent (default is 'default').
    game (Game): The local game instance used to mirror the remote game state.
    game_spec (dict): Specifications of the game (e.g., number of vertices, edges).
    host_url (str): The URL of the remote game server.
    token (dict): Authentication tokens for the remote game server.
    game_id (str): The ID of the game on the remote server.
    join_token (str): The token used to join the game.
    player_index (int): The index of the player in the game.

functions.name: tangled_agent.config_handler.setup_args
documentation: Set up handling of command-line arguments.

functions.name: tangled_agent.config_handler.add_custom_args
documentation:
Add a hook to add custom arguments to the argument parser.

Args:
    hook (callable): A function that accepts an ArgumentParser instance. This should add any arguments needed by the agent.

functions.name: tangled_agent.config_handler.parse_custom_args
documentation:
Run all hooks to add custom arguments to the argument parser.

Args:
    parser (ArgumentParser): The argument parser to add arguments to.

classes.name: tangled_agent.connection.api_connection.ConnectionClient

methods.name: tangled_agent.connection.api_connection.ConnectionClient.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the ConnectionClient with a specified host.

Args:
    host (str): The host address of the API.

methods.name: tangled_agent.connection.api_connection.ConnectionClient.__enter__
documentation:
Enters the runtime context for this object.

Returns:
    DefaultApi: The default API instance.

methods.name: tangled_agent.connection.api_connection.ConnectionClient.__exit__
documentation:
Exits the runtime context for this object.

Args:
    exc_type (Optional[Type[BaseException]]): The exception type.
    exc_val (Optional[BaseException]): The exception value.
    exc_tb (Optional[Any]): The traceback object.

A context manager for managing API client connections.

Attributes:
    host (str): The host address of the API.
    api_client (Optional[ApiClient]): The API client instance.
    api_instance (Optional[DefaultApi]): The default API instance.

modules.name: tangled_agent.connection
documentation: Support for connecting to the API and handling the authentication process.

methods.name: tangled_agent.connection.auth0_handler.Auth0DeviceAuthorization.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Auth0DeviceAuthorization class with environment variables.

Example of environment variables:
- AUTH0_CLIENT_ID: Your Auth0 Client ID
- AUTH0_SCOPE: Your Auth0 Scope (default: 'openid profile email')
- AUTH0_AUDIENCE: Your Auth0 API Audience
- AUTH0_DOMAIN: Your Auth0 Domain (e.g., your-domain.auth0.com)

methods.name: tangled_agent.connection.auth0_handler.Auth0DeviceAuthorization.start_device_authorization
documentation:
Start the device authorization process by making a request to Auth0.

Returns:
    bool: True if the request was successful, False otherwise.

methods.name: tangled_agent.connection.auth0_handler.Auth0DeviceAuthorization.prompt_user
documentation: Prompt the user to visit the verification URI and enter the user code.

methods.name: tangled_agent.connection.auth0_handler.Auth0DeviceAuthorization.poll_token
documentation:
Poll Auth0 for the authentication token.

Returns:
    Optional[Dict[str, Any]]: The token response if successful, None otherwise.

methods.name: tangled_agent.connection.auth0_handler.Auth0DeviceAuthorization.authenticate
documentation:
Perform the authentication process.

Returns:
    Optional[str]: The access token if authentication is successful, None otherwise.

modules.name: tangled_agent.local_game_player
documentation:
LocalGamePlayer class for playing a game locally.

This class is used to play a game locally between two agents. Use this to test your 
agents against each other without incurring server costs.

classes.name: tangled_agent.local_game_player.LocalGamePlayer

methods.name: tangled_agent.local_game_player.LocalGamePlayer.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the LocalGamePlayer with the two agents and the game to play.

Args:
    player1 (GameAgentBase): The first player agent.
    player2 (GameAgentBase): The second player agent.
    game (Game): The game to play.

methods.name: tangled_agent.local_game_player.LocalGamePlayer.play_game
documentation:
Play the game until it is over. Calls will be made by play_game to the agents
to select moves for their turn until the game is over.

Returns:
    dict: The final state of the game.

LocalGamePlayer class for playing a game locally between two agents.
Instantiate it with references to the two agents and the game to play.
Call play_game() to play the game until it is over. Calls will be made by
play_game to the agents to select moves for their turn until the game is over.
